author: vastea
description: Kanban, timeline and weekly-log views for tasks stored in your notes, powered by Bases. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.description: Kanban, timeline and weekly-log views for tasks stored in your notes, powered by Bases.
manifest.name: Task Manager Bases View

name: Task Manager Bases View

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## English

### Added
- **Calendar — edit a time block.** Right-click a block → **Edit block** reopens the log modal pre-filled with the block's time, description, linked task and category, and writes a full update back to the daily note. An inline category colour (e.g. `(Dev|#4c8bf5)`) is preserved when you save without changing the category.

### Fixed
- **Calendar — back-reference stays in sync.** Moving, resizing, deleting or editing a time block now updates (or removes) the matching record in the linked task, not just the daily note. Previously the back-reference was only written when a block was created, so it drifted out of sync or was left stale. Only relevant when "write back-reference into linked task" is enabled.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/vastea/obsidian-task-manager-bases-view/compare/0.5.1...0.6.0

---

## 中文

### 新增
- **日历 — 编辑时间块。** 右键时间块 → **编辑时间块**，会打开预填好的日志弹窗（时间、描述、关联任务、类型），保存后整行重写并同步回当天的日记。若类型带有内联颜色（如 `(Dev|#4c8bf5)`），在不改动类型的情况下保存会保留该颜色。

### 修复
- **日历 — 回链保持同步。** 移动、缩放、删除或编辑时间块时，现在会同步更新（或移除）被关联任务里对应的那条记录，而不再只改日记。此前回链只在创建时写入，因而会与时间块脱节或残留旧记录。仅在开启「回写引用到关联任务」时相关。

**完整更新日志：** https://github.com/vastea/obsidian-task-manager-bases-view/compare/0.5.1...0.6.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Weekly log now lives in your daily notes

The calendar creates and looks up daily notes through Obsidian's core **Daily notes** plugin instead of its own folder/format settings — one source of truth for where daily notes live and how they're named, with nested folders and your daily-note template applied automatically.

- The plugin's own **Journal folder** and **Date format** settings are gone; the folder, filename date format and template now come from **Settings → Core plugins → Daily notes**.
- Opening the weekly log shows a short reminder confirming the active folder / filename format / template, with a shortcut straight to those settings. **Don't remind again** dismisses it for good; closing it keeps the reminder for next time.

### Smaller touches

- **Live enable/disable.** Toggling the calendar in settings now adds or removes its ribbon icon and command immediately — no more disabling and re-enabling the plugin.
- **Compact short blocks.** Time blocks too short to stack render on a single line (time + ellipsised rest), with the full content in the hover tooltip.

### Heads up

You'll need the core **Daily notes** plugin enabled (the calendar shows a Notice if it isn't). If your old journal folder/format differs from your Daily notes config, set them to match so existing logs are still found.

Still requires Obsidian 1.10+.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/vastea/obsidian-task-manager-bases-view/compare/0.4.1...0.5.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's changed

This is a patch release focused on kanban card readability.

### Fixed
- **Card properties now show their names.** Each property renders as `name: value` (using the friendly name from the Base config), one property per line — previously cards showed bare values that were hard to tell apart.
- **Even row spacing.** Date and link values are flattened to the same font size and line height as text rows, so rows line up evenly instead of date rows standing out taller.
- Internal: removed an unsafe `any` in the changelog status normalisation.

### Changed
- **Card styling.** Added a divider between the card title and its properties, and bumped the card corner radius to match the column.

---

Presentation-only — no data model or write-back changes. Still requires Obsidian 1.10+.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/vastea/obsidian-task-manager-bases-view/compare/0.4.0...0.4.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Kanban: record a status-change changelog on card move
A new opt-in option logs every status change to the note body, so you get a per-task history of how it moved across the board.

- **Record changelog** (view option, off by default) — when on, moving a card (drag, or right-click → "Move to") appends a list item to the note.
- **Changelog section heading** (view option, default `Changelog`) — the body section the entries are written under; it's created automatically if the note doesn't have it yet.
- Entry format: `- yyyy-MM-dd old->new`, e.g. `- 2026-06-13 todo->done`.
- No entry is written when the status doesn't actually change.
